```json
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 90,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Cable shield is grounded at both ends, creating a ground loop.",
    "Ground loop can induce noise, erratic signals, and measurement errors in instrument loops.",
    "Condition violates typical loop drawing and ISA 5.1 shield grounding practice (single-point ground at control room end for analog signal cables).",
    "Loop check documentation may not reflect actual field condition."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove shield ground at field end, leaving single-point ground at control room or designated ground point per loop drawing.",
    "Re-verify shield continuity and ground configuration with multimeter/continuity tester.",
    "Re-perform loop check and document corrected configuration.",
    "Update as-built drawings and loop check records."
  ],
  "rationale": "Grounding a cable shield at both ends creates a ground loop, which is a known source of noise and signal instability in instrumentation loops. Standard practice and typical loop drawings require single-point shield grounding, usually at the control room or marshalling cabinet end. This condition is non-compliant and must be corrected before the loop can be accepted.",
  "references": [
    "loop drawings",
    "ISA 5.1"
  ]
}
```
